title: deferred Lua callback leaks a libuv timer handle when scheduling fails at exit
category: resource leak (libuv timer handle never closed; Lua stdlib ↔ C uv loop)
crash_site: Neovim reports leaked libuv handles / does not shut down cleanly after a timer-based deferred Lua callback is registered shortly before exit. The leak report points at the uv loop, not at the registering code.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: vim.schedule (the C-backed scheduler in executor.c that can fail) and the uv timer creation. The defect is defer_fn not closing its timer when schedule fails.
ground_truth.fix_summary: vim.defer_fn() creates a libuv timer and, on fire, calls vim.schedule(). If vim.schedule() fails (e.g. during shutdown), defer_fn never closed the timer -> leaked luv handle. Fix: make vim.schedule() return an error when scheduling fails, and make vim.defer_fn() close the timer when vim.schedule() failed.

admission.note: Authored from PR #37647 body+diff. crash != cause: the symptom is a leaked uv handle at exit; the cause is defer_fn (editor.lua) not closing its timer when the C-backed vim.schedule() fails. Decoy = vim.schedule. Resource-leak category (not a crash) -> moderate C5; valuable for Lua coverage. Lua stdlib <-> C uv loop. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37647 (fixes #19727) — close vim.defer_fn() timer if vim.schedule() failed; fix 1906da52dbc9, base 0501c5fd0959.
